terms.definition: The short hand on a clock that shows the hour. It moves slowly and goes around the clock twice in one full day.
terms.exampleUsage: When the hour hand points to 5, it means the hour is 5.

terms.definition: The long hand on a clock that shows the minutes. It moves faster than the hour hand and goes around the clock once every hour.
terms.exampleUsage: When the minute hand points to 3, it means 15 minutes past the hour.

terms.definition: The time from midnight (12:00 at night) to just before noon (12:00 in the afternoon). AM stands for the morning hours.
terms.exampleUsage: School assembly starts at 8:30 AM, which is in the morning.

terms.definition: The time from noon (12:00 in the afternoon) to just before midnight (12:00 at night). PM covers the afternoon, evening, and night hours.
terms.exampleUsage: We eat dinner at about 8:00 PM, which is in the evening.

terms.definition: The amount of time that passes between a start time and an end time. It tells how long something lasted.
terms.exampleUsage: A class starts at 10:00 AM and ends at 10:40 AM. The elapsed time is 40 minutes.

terms.definition: A chart that shows the days, weeks, and months of a year. It helps us keep track of dates and plan events.
terms.exampleUsage: I checked the calendar and found that Diwali falls on a Thursday this year.

terms.definition: A year that has 366 days instead of 365. February has 29 days in a leap year. A leap year comes once every 4 years.
terms.exampleUsage: The year 2024 is a leap year, so February has 29 days instead of 28.

terms.definition: 12 o'clock in the middle of the day. It is when AM changes to PM. Also called midday.
terms.exampleUsage: Lunch break at school is at noon, which is 12:00 PM.

terms.definition: 12 o'clock in the middle of the night. It is when PM changes to AM. A new day begins at midnight.
terms.exampleUsage: Most people are sleeping at midnight, which is 12:00 AM.

terms.definition: 15 minutes after the hour. The minute hand points to 3 on the clock face.
terms.exampleUsage: Quarter past 9 means 9:15. The long hand is on 3 and the short hand is just past 9.

terms.definition: 30 minutes after the hour. The minute hand points to 6 on the clock face.
terms.exampleUsage: Half past 3 means 3:30. The long hand is on 6 and the short hand is between 3 and 4.

terms.definition: 15 minutes before the next hour, which means 45 minutes past the current hour. The minute hand points to 9.
terms.exampleUsage: Quarter to 5 means 4:45. It is 15 minutes before 5 o'clock.
